mission.title: argo: Add option to create one-off job from cron job
mission.description: Add option to create one-off job from cron job. This issue affects 163+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Add option to create one-off job from cron job

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
Add a button to create a one-off job from a cron job.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add option to create one-off job from cron job
steps.description:
@muenchdo @genslein 
Basically, the result of performing a "kubectl create job --from=cronjob/foo foo" is : k8s creating a job resource immediately.
The job manifest is yet another k8s resource.

My suggestion: handling the imperative "kubectl create" result as a declarative representation of this o
```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  ...
```

steps.title: Confirm Add option to create one-off job from cron job is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: @muenchdo @genslein 
Basically, the result of performing a "kubectl create job --from=cronjob/foo foo" is : k8s creating a job resource immediately.
The job manifest is yet another k8s resource.

My suggestion: handling the imperative "kubectl create" result as a declarative representation of this one-time job resource and dealing with it in a regular GitOps way.

Can the following work for you:
-. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
